name: Dried Sour Cherry Nut Clusters
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces high quality white, milk, or bittersweet chocolate, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a sheet tray with parchment or wax paper.
2. Temper the chocolate by melting about 2/3 of it in the microwave on low power, stirring occasionally to ensure it does not burn (approximately 1 1/2 minutes). Alternatively, melt 2/3 of the chopped chocolate over simmering water, ensuring no water touches the chocolate. Stir in the remaining chopped chocolate and continue stirring until fully melted.
3. Test the temperature by touching the skin just below your lower lip; it should feel cool to the touch. If necessary, add a large chunk of chocolate and stir to cool further.
4. Stir in 2/3 cup of toasted almonds and 2/3 cup of dried sour cherries.
5. Using an ice cream scoop or spoon, drop clusters of the chocolate mixture onto the prepared sheet tray. Place an almond and one or two dried cherries on top of each cluster (not all remaining ingredients may be used).
6. Refrigerate until set for at least 15 minutes.
7. Gently lift the clusters with a flexible knife.
8. Store in a cool, dry place.
Yield: 20 to 24 pieces
